text.title: Technology Stack Selection Decision Tree Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the decision-tree-tech-stack methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: When team expertise in a candidate stack is concentrated (≥70% of engineers have ≥1 year hands-on), that stack MUST be the default unless a specific workload disqualifies it.
rule.rationale: Hiring + ramp-up cost dwarfs language-feature differences; ignoring team expertise produces 6-12 month productivity dips.

rule.statement: When team expertise is neutral, route by workload niche: real-time / numeric → Go / Rust, AI / data → Python, browser / SSR → TypeScript, JVM-shop / regulated → Java / Kotlin. Niche routing MUST follow r1, never override it.

rationale.source: Stack Overflow 2025 dev survey; language-by-niche capability matrix.

rule.rationale: Each language retains a workload niche where standard-library + ecosystem alignment shortens delivery by months.

rule.statement: Adopting a bleeding-edge framework (< 2 years GA, < 1k production case studies) MUST carry an explicit risk budget (engineering weeks reserved for upstream-blocking bugs) and a fallback plan.
rule.rationale: Bleeding-edge frameworks burn engineering time on bugs the user community has not yet flushed out; teams without budget burn out.

rule.statement: Datastore choice MUST follow data shape (relational entities → Postgres, document/JSON-heavy → Postgres-JSONB or Mongo, time-series → TimescaleDB / ClickHouse, vector → pgvector / Qdrant). Datastore picked for popularity instead of data shape is a defect.
rule.rationale: Datastore-data-shape mismatch produces 10× query complexity and 3-5× operational cost; the data shape is the determinant.

rule.statement: Stack ADR MUST list rejected alternatives with reasons, MUST include a review trigger (hiring failure rate, perf miss, ecosystem shift), and MUST be reviewed annually.
rule.rationale: Without rejected-alternatives + trigger, the next architect cannot reconstruct the reasoning; review is the safety net for shifting markets.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (expertise, performance target, hiring data, ecosystem deps), walk r1 → r4 → r2 → r3 → r5 and emit the ADR.
rule.rationale: Out-of-order evaluation produces team-incompatible picks; the order encodes risk reduction.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) platform mandate exists, (b) it is a library swap inside a service, (c) the project is throwaway. Note the constraint and exit.
rule.rationale: Stack ceremony for constrained or short-lived choices wastes review time.
